prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
Here are four sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler documenting SaaS Q3 results:

---

"In the third quarter of the year of our Lord, the wizards of the Cloud did conjure forth a Monthly Recurring Revenue of four and thirty millions, which is most pleasing unto the shareholders, though I confess I know not what a shareholder is and suspect it may be a kind of miller."

"The churn rate, which I understand to be not a device for making butter but rather a measure of fleeing customers, did decrease by two points, and the scribes in the chamber called Finance did weep with joy, though their faces remained as still and pale as men awaiting plague."

"A great army of one thousand new enterprise accounts did swear fealty to the platform in Q3, pledging their gold on an annual basis, which is a more honorable arrangement than monthly, as any serf paying rent would understand."

"I have been told that the product called the Dashboard requires no horses and yet allows a man in one kingdom to observe numbers from another kingdom in real time, which I have recorded faithfully in this chronicle while privately concluding that someone in this company has made a terrible arrangement with the Devil."
